commit.message:
Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

The lint test checks per-line for search_skills()/list_skills() references
and only exempts lines containing "do not" or "never call". The prohibition
was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

Add dashboard tool output rendering, shell-server live logs, token budget principle

commit.message:
Add pivot-aware scanning to network-recon, improve dashboard agent lifecycle

network-recon skill:
- Add "Pivot Mode" section with two-phase scanning approach
- Phase 1: fast host discovery on pivot host (ARP, ping sweep, DNS zone,
  single-port proxychains sweep)
- Phase 2: targeted nmap through proxychains on known-live hosts only
- Document nmap MCP server limitation (no proxychains support)
- Add static nmap alternative for speed-critical scenarios
- Add pivot-specific troubleshooting entries

Dashboard (tail-agent.py):
- Auto-discover new agents from tasks directory (no .dashboard file needed)
- Red *stopped* indicator in pane header for dead agents
- Detect agent completion via JSONL final message + 5s mtime debounce
- Detect killed agents (TaskStop) via 30s mtime staleness
- Keep stopped agents visible instead of auto-removing
- Space bar toggles agents in/out from browser overlay

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Update docs: CLAUDE.md, state-server README, orchestrator routing tables

commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Dashboard: only show active agents, auto-remove completed

Active agents are detected by output file mtime (60s threshold).
Completed agents are automatically removed from the dashboard.
Completed agents remain accessible via the agent browser (b key).
If no agents are active, the dashboard sits idle until one spawns.

Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

commit.message:
fix(cli): add type annotation to auto-generated resolveType parameter (#194)

## Why

The auto-generated `resolveType` function parameter lacked a type
annotation, causing an implicit `any` type error when users have
`noImplicitAny` enabled in their TypeScript config.

## Summary

- Added type annotation to the generated `resolveType` parameter using
the parent type name (e.g., `(obj: SearchResult)`)
- Updated golden test snapshots to reflect the new type annotations in
generated resolver files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use <a> instead of <Link> for cross-zone routes to learnvibecoding

Routes like /curriculum, /quiz, /discover, /claude-code, /concepts are
proxied to learnvibecoding via Vercel rewrites. Next.js <Link> tries to
fetch RSC payloads for these, which fails and falls back to browser
navigation. Using plain <a> tags avoids the failed fetch entirely.

commit.message:
chore: generate golden-main stabilization phase 2 reports (#18886)

Generated the five requested deliverables to assist with the Golden-Main
Stabilization Phase 2:
1. `ci-determinism-report.md`: Identifies workflows potentially causing
duplicate CI runs and validates content-addressable cache keys.
2. `mbv-violations.json`: Mock output array for the Modular Boundary
Verifier (currently empty).
3. `governance-merge-audit.md`: A PASS/FAIL checklist matrix analyzing
active PRs for audit hooks, feature flags, and policy test coverage.
4. `resilience-checks.md`: Validation report for Redis graceful
degradation, offline-first boot capability, and backup/restore
idempotency.
5. `perf-regression-report.md`: Analysis of N+1 query eliminations,
Redis partitioning overhead, and benchmark reproducibility.

All deliverables were placed in the `reports/` directory per the prompt
instructions.

commit.message:
Replace OTP flow with magic-link email verification

Supabase project sends email links, not 6-digit codes. Updated the full
auth flow to match:

- request-otp route: add emailRedirectTo=/auth/callback so Supabase
  embeds the correct callback URL in the verification email
- app/auth/callback/page.tsx (new): client page that processes the magic
  link tokens from the URL hash, then redirects to
  /paywall?step=stripe&userId=...&email=...
- paywall/page.tsx: remove OTP step entirely; new 'link_sent' step shows
  "We sent a confirmation link to your email. Click it to verify." with
  a Resend button; 'stripe' step now activates when callback redirects
  back with verified userId + email as URL params; wrapped in Suspense
  for useSearchParams

commit.message:
feat: implement SSCP-LU-DATA support for real z/OS hosts

Real z/OS hosts send SSCP-LU-DATA (TN3270E data type 7) for the VTAM
logon screen before binding a 3270 session. Previously this threw an
error, causing blank screen with SYSTEM LOCKED. Now handles it by
displaying raw EBCDIC text on an unformatted screen and sending user
input back as SSCP-LU-DATA when Enter is pressed.

commit.message:
fix: relax signal gates — ADX penalty, sub-score veto 40, multi-candle soft gate

Signals were never reaching 'active' status due to 5 compounding hard gates.
Converted ADX>=25 hard gate to -4/-8pt penalty, lowered sub-score veto from
>50 to >40, relaxed MACD consistency to 2/4 candles, made multi-candle
confirmation a -5pt penalty instead of hard block. Technical-only threshold
lowered from 75 to 70. Added rejection reasons to dashboard SignalCard.

commit.message:
feat(cli): use Rust oxc_linter crate instead of npm oxlint binary (#52)

## Summary

- Replace the `rex lint` command's shell-out to the `oxlint` npm binary
with a native Rust implementation using the `oxc_linter` crate (same
pattern as `rex fmt` using `oxc_formatter`)
- Add `--deny-warnings` flag and explicit path positional args (e.g.
`rex lint runtime/ packages/rex/src/`)
- Remove `oxlint` npm dependency from `packages/rex/package.json`
- Update CI workflow to use `cargo run -- lint` instead of
`oxc-project/oxlint-action`
- Update lefthook pre-commit hook to use the built-in linter

## Motivation

The `rex fmt` command already uses the `oxc_formatter` Rust crate
directly rather than shelling out to a Node.js binary. This PR brings
`rex lint` to parity by using `oxc_linter` natively, eliminating the npm
dependency on `oxlint` and making linting work out of the box without
requiring Node.js or npm.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check --all-targets` — zero warnings
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test` — all 238 tests pass
- [x] `cargo run -- lint --root fixtures/basic` — detects lint warnings
- [x] `cargo run -- lint --deny-warnings --root fixtures/basic` — exits
with code 1 on warnings
- [x] `cargo run -- lint runtime/ packages/rex/src/` — lints explicit
paths

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: claude-liminal <264858718+claude-liminal@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Austin Montoya <126262824+austin-liminal@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Willis 비율 공식을 논문 원법으로 수정

- 비율 공식: (동공~비하점)/(비하점~턱끝) → (비하점~턱끝)/(동공~구열)
- 비하점 랜드마크: 인덱스 2 → 94 (정확한 subnasale 위치)
- 거리 계산: Y축만 → 2D 유클리드 거리 + aspect ratio 보정
- 정상 범위: 0.9~1.5 → 0.85~1.15 (논문 기준 1:1 ± 15%)
- 오버레이: 동공~구열(녹색) + 비하점~턱끝(주황) 시각화

commit.message:
Fix fair_p gap: drift horizon cap, vol floor, entry condition improvements

Probability model fixes (binance_strategy.py, binance_feed.py):
- Add DRIFT_HORIZON_SEC=120: cap momentum projection at 2 min.
  Autocorrelation in 1-min BTC decays within 2-3 min; extrapolating
  further over 7+ min was pushing E[A] past strike → P=52% when
  Kalshi said 32%.
- Raise CRYPTO_VOL_FLOOR to 40%: prevents 9%-vol estimate from
  giving P=0% when Kalshi prices 18%. BTC options implied vol rarely
  drops below 35-40% even at market troughs.
- Add get_long_run_vol(): avg of _vols deque (~96 min history, ~7% error).
- 3-way blend: 10% 5-min + 30% 30-min + 60% long-run (+ floor).
- Simplify _gbm_avg_mean_var to zero-drift; drift handled by
  _drift_adjusted_s0() with DRIFT_HORIZON_SEC cap.

Net results on observed dashboard scenarios:
  vol=9%  price-$108 6.0min:  0.0% → 10.3%  (was broken)
  vol=22% price -$58 7.8min: 16.5% → 35.2%  (Kalshi 32.5%)
  vol=45% price +$88 3.4min: 75.0% → 95.0%  (Kalshi 96.5%)

Entry condition fixes (hft_bot.py):
- Remove vol regime filter from compute_signal: high vol → small z →
  less edge; filter was blocking the best trades (low vol, far from strike)
- Raise edge sanity cap 50c → 75c: 50c was rejecting cheap contracts
  (21-30c ask) where model has high conviction and 50c+ edge is real
- Raise MAX_SPREAD 6c → 8c: hold-to-expiry only pays ask (not bid
  on exit), so spread cost at exit is zero; 8c still filters illiquid markets
- Fix limit price int() → round(): truncation was losing ~1c per order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix distinct_id mismatch between server flag eval and client PostHog

Root cause: server used a random UUID for flag evaluation, client PostHog
generated its own UUID — two separate identities in PostHog, so exposure
events never linked to pageviews/conversions.

- Add middleware.ts to set a persistent aura_vid cookie before any render
- page.tsx reads aura_vid for flag evaluation
- layout.tsx reads aura_vid and passes to PostHogProvider
- PostHogProvider bootstraps posthog.init() with distinctID so client
  uses the same identity as the server-side exposure event

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add visual overhaul, attack bribe system, and edge wall barriers

Major renderer rewrite with offscreen canvas caching, procedural island/ocean
rendering with palm trees, coral, docks, and depth-aware water effects. Add
attack bribe system allowing defenders to offer resources to cancel attacks,
with honor and ruthless modes. Implement edge-based wall barriers that block
ship movement between adjacent sea tiles, placed near islands and board edges
to hinder treasure collection. Fix board density by making fixDeadEnds() prefer
opening paths over filling sea and adding 55% minimum sea validation.
